title: The Mailbox Nobody Uses
text_zh: 我们小区的一楼有一排信箱，但是大部分都生锈了。现在谁还寄信呢？但是有一个信箱比较干净——三零二室的。我注意到每个星期三下午，有一个年轻人会来开那个信箱，拿出一封信。有一天我忍不住问他：「还有人给你寄信吗？」他笑着说：「是我自己给自己寄的。我每个星期写一封信，写下这个星期发生的事情和我的想法，然后寄给自己。一个星期以后收到的时候，我就可以用另一种角度看自己了。」我觉得这比较有意思——给未来的自己写信，就像跟一个星期以后的自己聊天。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen xiǎoqū de yī lóu yǒu yì pái xìnxiāng, dànshì dàbùfen dōu shēng xiù le. Xiànzài shéi hái jì xìn ne? Dànshì yǒu yí gè xìnxiāng bǐjiào gānjìng——sān líng èr shì de. Wǒ zhùyì dào měi gè xīngqī sān xiàwǔ, yǒu yí gè niánqīng rén huì lái kāi nàge xìnxiāng, ná chū yì fēng xìn. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ rěn bú zhù wèn tā:「Hái yǒu rén gěi nǐ jì xìn ma?」Tā xiàozhe shuō:「Shì wǒ zìjǐ gěi zìjǐ jì de. Wǒ měi gè xīngqī xiě yì fēng xìn, xiě xià zhège xīngqī fāshēng de shìqing hé wǒ de xiǎngfǎ, ránhòu jì gěi zìjǐ. Yí gè xīngqī yǐhòu shōu dào de shíhou, wǒ jiù kěyǐ yòng lìng yì zhǒng jiǎodù kàn zìjǐ le.」Wǒ juéde zhè bǐjiào yǒuyìsi——gěi wèilái de zìjǐ xiě xìn, jiù xiàng gēn yí gè xīngqī yǐhòu de zìjǐ liáotiān.
text_en: On the first floor of our complex there's a row of mailboxes, but most are rusty. Who sends letters anymore? Yet one mailbox is rather clean — unit 302's. I noticed every Wednesday afternoon, a young man opens it and takes out a letter. One day I asked: 'Does someone still write to you?' He smiled: 'I write to myself. Every week I write a letter about what happened and my thoughts, then mail it to myself. When I receive it a week later, I can see myself from a different angle.' I found this rather interesting — writing to your future self is like chatting with yourself a week from now.

questions.q_zh: 大部分信箱怎么样了？
questions.q_en: What happened to most mailboxes?

questions.q_zh: 那个年轻人的信是谁寄的？
questions.q_en: Who sends the young man's letters?

options.pinyin: tā zìjǐ gěi zìjǐ jì de

questions.q_zh: 他为什么这样做？
questions.q_en: Why does he do this?

options.text: 想用另一种角度看自己
options.pinyin: xiǎng yòng lìng yì zhǒng jiǎodù kàn zìjǐ
options.text_en: wants to see himself from a different angle

title: The Bicycle Lane
text_zh: 我们城市去年新修了一条自行车道，从城东一直到城西。刚修好的时候，很少有人骑。大家还是习惯开车或者坐地铁。但是慢慢地，越来越多的人开始骑自行车了。早上七八点的时候，自行车道上全是骑车上班的人。有一个同事以前每天开车上班要四十分钟，现在骑自行车只要三十分钟，而且不用找停车位。他说：「骑车的时候能感觉到风，还能看到路边的花。开车的时候什么都看不到，只能看到前面的车。」自行车道旁边种了很多树，春天的时候骑在上面，比较舒服。有时候慢一点，反而能看到更多。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen chéngshì qùnián xīn xiū le yì tiáo zìxíngchē dào, cóng chéng dōng yìzhí dào chéng xī. Gāng xiū hǎo de shíhou, hěn shǎo yǒu rén qí. Dàjiā háishi xíguàn kāi chē huòzhě zuò dìtiě. Dànshì mànman de, yuè lái yuè duō de rén kāishǐ qí zìxíngchē le. Zǎoshang qī bā diǎn de shíhou, zìxíngchē dào shàng quán shì qí chē shàngbān de rén. Yǒu yí gè tóngshì yǐqián měi tiān kāi chē shàngbān yào sìshí fēnzhōng, xiànzài qí zìxíngchē zhǐ yào sānshí fēnzhōng, érqiě bú yòng zhǎo tíng chē wèi. Tā shuō:「Qí chē de shíhou néng gǎnjué dào fēng, hái néng kàn dào lù biān de huā. Kāi chē de shíhou shénme dōu kàn bú dào, zhǐ néng kàn dào qiánmiàn de chē.」Zìxíngchē dào pángbiān zhòng le hěn duō shù, chūntiān de shíhou qí zài shàngmiàn, bǐjiào shūfu. Yǒu shíhou màn yìdiǎn, fǎn'ér néng kàn dào gèng duō.
text_en: Our city built a new bike lane last year, running from east to west. When first completed, few people rode. Everyone was still used to driving or taking the subway. But gradually more and more people started cycling. At seven or eight in the morning, the bike lane is full of commuters. One colleague used to drive forty minutes to work — now she bikes in thirty, and doesn't need to find parking. She says: 'When you bike you feel the wind, you see flowers along the road. When you drive you see nothing except the car ahead.' The bike lane has many trees planted alongside it — riding in spring is rather comfortable. Sometimes going slower, you actually see more.

questions.q_zh: 自行车道从哪里到哪里？
questions.q_en: Where does the bike lane go?

options.pinyin: cóng chéng běi dào chéng nán
cóng chéng dōng dào chéng xī

questions.q_zh: 同事为什么改骑自行车？
questions.q_en: Why did the colleague switch to cycling?

options.text: 骑车比开车快，还不用找停车位
options.pinyin: qí chē bǐ kāi chē kuài, hái bú yòng zhǎo tíng chē wèi
options.text_en: faster than driving, no need for parking

questions.q_zh: 最后一句话是什么意思？
questions.q_en: What does the last sentence mean?

options.text: 慢一点反而能看到更多
options.pinyin: màn yìdiǎn fǎn'ér néng kàn dào gèng duō
options.text_en: going slower lets you see more

title: The Birthday Surprise
text_zh: 今天是同事小王的生日。我们几个人决定给他一个惊喜。计划很简单：中午的时候，我们假装去开会，把他一个人留在办公室。然后带着蛋糕回来，唱生日歌。但是出了两个问题。第一，蛋糕上的名字被写错了——写成了「小黄」，不是「小王」。第二，我们回到办公室的时候，小王已经出去吃午饭了。我们只好带着蛋糕去餐厅找他。他正在吃面。我们端着蛋糕进去的时候，整个餐厅的人都在看我们。小王先是愣了一下，然后看到蛋糕上的名字，笑得差点把面喷出来。他说：「这是我收到的最好笑的惊喜。」
text_pinyin: Jīntiān shì tóngshì Xiǎo Wáng de shēngrì. Wǒmen jǐ gè rén juédìng gěi tā yí gè jīngxǐ. Jìhuà hěn jiǎndān: zhōngwǔ de shíhou, wǒmen jiǎzhuāng qù kāi huì, bǎ tā yí gè rén liú zài bàngōngshì. Ránhòu dàizhe dàngāo huílái, chàng shēngrì gē. Dànshì chū le liǎng gè wèntí. Dì-yī, dàngāo shàng de míngzi bèi xiě cuò le——xiě chéng le「Xiǎo Huáng」, búshì「Xiǎo Wáng」. Dì-èr, wǒmen huí dào bàngōngshì de shíhou, Xiǎo Wáng yǐjīng chūqù chī wǔfàn le. Wǒmen zhǐhǎo dàizhe dàngāo qù cāntīng zhǎo tā. Tā zhèngzài chī miàn. Wǒmen duānzhe dàngāo jìnqù de shíhou, zhěng gè cāntīng de rén dōu zài kàn wǒmen. Xiǎo Wáng xiān shì lèng le yíxià, ránhòu kàn dào dàngāo shàng de míngzi, xiào de chàdiǎn bǎ miàn pēn chūlái. Tā shuō:「Zhè shì wǒ shōu dào de zuì hǎoxiào de jīngxǐ.」
text_en: Today is colleague Xiao Wang's birthday. A few of us decided to surprise him. Simple plan: at noon, we'd pretend to go to a meeting, leaving him alone. Then come back with cake and sing. But two things went wrong. First, the name on the cake was wrong — it said 'Xiao Huang' instead of 'Xiao Wang.' Second, when we returned to the office, Xiao Wang had already gone out for lunch. We had to take the cake to the restaurant to find him. He was eating noodles. When we walked in carrying the cake, the entire restaurant stared. Xiao Wang was stunned for a moment, then saw the name on the cake and nearly spat out his noodles laughing. He said: 'This is the funniest surprise I've ever gotten.'

questions.q_zh: 蛋糕上的名字写的是什么？
questions.q_en: What name was on the cake?

questions.q_zh: 他们回到办公室发现了什么？
questions.q_en: What did they find when they returned to the office?

options.text: 小王已经出去吃午饭了
options.pinyin: Xiǎo Wáng yǐjīng chūqù chī wǔfàn le
options.text_en: Xiao Wang had gone to lunch
Xiao Wang was in a meeting

questions.q_zh: 小王觉得这个惊喜怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did Xiao Wang feel about the surprise?

title: The Old Man and His Camera
text_zh: 公园里有一个老人，每天都带着一台很旧的相机来拍照。他不拍风景，只拍人。他拍在长椅上看书的人，拍在草地上玩的孩子，拍跳广场舞的阿姨们。有人问他：「为什么不拍风景？树和花比较好看啊。」他说：「花明年还会开，但是今天这个在椅子上睡着的老人，明年不一定还在这里。」他把拍的照片打印出来，送给被拍的人。有一次他给一个带孩子的妈妈送了一张照片。照片里她正在给孩子擦嘴，笑得很温柔。她看了以后红了眼眶，说：「平时都是我给别人拍照，从来没有人帮我拍过这样的照片。」
text_pinyin: Gōngyuán lǐ yǒu yí gè lǎorén, měi tiān dōu dàizhe yì tái hěn jiù de xiàngjī lái pāi zhào. Tā bù pāi fēngjǐng, zhǐ pāi rén. Tā pāi zài cháng yǐ shàng kàn shū de rén, pāi zài cǎodì shàng wán de háizi, pāi tiào guǎngchǎng wǔ de āyímen. Yǒu rén wèn tā:「Wèishénme bù pāi fēngjǐng? Shù hé huā bǐjiào hǎokàn a.」Tā shuō:「Huā míngnián hái huì kāi, dànshì jīntiān zhège zài yǐzi shàng shuìzháo de lǎorén, míngnián bù yídìng hái zài zhèlǐ.」Tā bǎ pāi de zhàopiàn dǎyìn chūlái, sòng gěi bèi pāi de rén. Yǒu yí cì tā gěi yí gè dài háizi de māma sòng le yì zhāng zhàopiàn. Zhàopiàn lǐ tā zhèngzài gěi háizi cā zuǐ, xiào de hěn wēnróu. Tā kàn le yǐhòu hóng le yǎnkuàng, shuō:「Píngshí dōu shì wǒ gěi biéren pāi zhào, cónglái méiyǒu rén bāng wǒ pāi guò zhèyàng de zhàopiàn.」
text_en: An old man comes to the park every day with a very old camera to take photos. He doesn't photograph scenery — only people. He photographs people reading on benches, children playing on grass, aunties doing square dancing. Someone asked: 'Why not photograph scenery? Trees and flowers are prettier.' He said: 'Flowers will bloom again next year, but this old man sleeping on the bench may not be here next year.' He prints his photos and gives them to his subjects. Once he gave a photo to a mother with her child. In it she was wiping her child's mouth, smiling tenderly. She teared up and said: 'Usually I'm the one taking photos of others. Nobody has ever taken a photo like this of me.'

questions.q_zh: 老人拍什么？
questions.q_en: What does the old man photograph?

questions.q_zh: 老人为什么不拍风景？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't the old man photograph scenery?

options.text: 花明年还会开，但人不一定还在
options.pinyin: huā míngnián hái huì kāi, dàn rén bù yídìng hái zài
options.text_en: flowers bloom again, but people may not be there

options.pinyin: tā de xiàngjī pāi bù liǎo fēngjǐng
options.text_en: his camera can't take scenery photos

questions.q_zh: 那个妈妈为什么红了眼眶？
questions.q_en: Why did the mother tear up?

options.text_en: she didn't want to be photographed

options.text: 从来没有人帮她拍过这样的照片
options.pinyin: cónglái méiyǒu rén bāng tā pāi guò zhèyàng de zhàopiàn
options.text_en: nobody had ever taken a photo like this of her

options.pinyin: tā xiǎng qǐ le zìjǐ de māma

title: The Library's Lost and Found
text_zh: 我们学校的图书馆有一个失物招领柜。里面放着学生们丢的各种东西：雨伞、水杯、耳机、手套。管理员张阿姨每天整理一次。她有一个特别的习惯——如果一个东西放了超过一个月还没有人来拿，她就会在上面贴一张纸条：「我在这里等你一个月了。」她说：「也许有人看到纸条会想起来。」有一次一个女生来拿一把粉色的雨伞，看到纸条以后笑了，说：「对不起，让你等了。」张阿姨说：「这里除了东西以外，还放着很多人的粗心。但是粗心没关系，只要记得回来拿就行。」
text_pinyin: Wǒmen xuéxiào de túshūguǎn yǒu yí gè shīwù zhāolǐng guì. Lǐmiàn fàngzhe xuéshēngmen diū de gè zhǒng dōngxi: yǔsǎn, shuǐ bēi, ěrjī, shǒutào. Guǎnlǐyuán Zhāng āyí měi tiān zhěnglǐ yí cì. Tā yǒu yí gè tèbié de xíguàn——rúguǒ yí gè dōngxi fàng le chāoguò yí gè yuè hái méiyǒu rén lái ná, tā jiù huì zài shàngmiàn tiē yì zhāng zhǐtiáo:「Wǒ zài zhèlǐ děng nǐ yí gè yuè le.」Tā shuō:「Yěxǔ yǒu rén kàn dào zhǐtiáo huì xiǎng qǐlái.」Yǒu yí cì yí gè nǚshēng lái ná yì bǎ fěnsè de yǔsǎn, kàn dào zhǐtiáo yǐhòu xiào le, shuō:「Duìbuqǐ, ràng nǐ děng le.」Zhāng āyí shuō:「Zhèlǐ chúle dōngxi yǐwài, hái fàngzhe hěn duō rén de cūxīn. Dànshì cūxīn méi guānxi, zhǐyào jìde huílái ná jiù xíng.」
text_en: Our school library has a lost and found cabinet. It holds all kinds of things students lose: umbrellas, water bottles, earphones, gloves. Attendant Auntie Zhang organizes it once a day. She has a special habit — if something goes unclaimed for over a month, she sticks a note on it: 'I've been waiting here for you for a month.' She says: 'Maybe someone will see the note and remember.' Once a girl came for a pink umbrella and saw the note. She laughed: 'Sorry for making you wait.' Auntie Zhang said: 'Besides lost items, this place holds a lot of people's carelessness. But carelessness is fine — as long as you remember to come back.'

questions.q_zh: 张阿姨在什么上面贴纸条？
questions.q_en: What does Auntie Zhang stick notes on?

options.text: 放了超过一个月没人拿的东西
options.pinyin: fàng le chāoguò yí gè yuè méi rén ná de dōngxi
options.text_en: items unclaimed for over a month

questions.q_zh: 纸条上写了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the note say?

options.text: 我在这里等你一个月了
options.pinyin: wǒ zài zhèlǐ děng nǐ yí gè yuè le
options.text_en: I've been waiting for you for a month

options.pinyin: zhège dōngxi yào bèi rēng le
options.text_en: this item will be thrown away

questions.q_zh: 张阿姨觉得这里除了东西还放着什么？
questions.q_en: What does Auntie Zhang think is stored here besides items?

title: The Rice Grandmother Made
text_zh: 我外婆做的米饭跟别人的不一样。她不用电饭锅，用的是一个很旧的铁锅。她先把米洗三遍，然后放水——水刚好到她手指第一个关节的位置。她说这是她妈妈教她的。用铁锅做的米饭底下会有一层锅巴，又脆又香。小时候我最喜欢吃锅巴。外婆每次都把锅巴留给我，自己吃上面比较软的饭。我长大以后在城市里工作，用电饭锅做饭，味道总是比较一般。有一次我回家，外婆又给我做了铁锅饭。我吃了一口锅巴，忽然觉得眼睛比较酸。不是因为好吃，是因为那个味道让我想起了小时候的厨房，和站在厨房里的外婆。
text_pinyin: Wǒ wàipó zuò de mǐfàn gēn biéren de bù yíyàng. Tā bú yòng diàn fàn guō, yòng de shì yí gè hěn jiù de tiě guō. Tā xiān bǎ mǐ xǐ sān biàn, ránhòu fàng shuǐ——shuǐ gānghǎo dào tā shǒuzhǐ dì-yī gè guānjié de wèizhì. Tā shuō zhè shì tā māma jiāo tā de. Yòng tiě guō zuò de mǐfàn dǐxia huì yǒu yì céng guōba, yòu cuì yòu xiāng. Xiǎo shíhou wǒ zuì xǐhuan chī guōba. Wàipó měi cì dōu bǎ guōba liú gěi wǒ, zìjǐ chī shàngmiàn bǐjiào ruǎn de fàn. Wǒ zhǎng dà yǐhòu zài chéngshì lǐ gōngzuò, yòng diàn fàn guō zuò fàn, wèidào zǒng shì bǐjiào yìbān. Yǒu yí cì wǒ huí jiā, wàipó yòu gěi wǒ zuò le tiě guō fàn. Wǒ chī le yì kǒu guōba, hūrán juéde yǎnjīng bǐjiào suān. Búshì yīnwèi hǎochī, shì yīnwèi nàge wèidào ràng wǒ xiǎng qǐ le xiǎo shíhou de chúfáng, hé zhàn zài chúfáng lǐ de wàipó.
text_en: My maternal grandmother makes rice differently from everyone else. She doesn't use a rice cooker — she uses a very old iron pot. She washes the rice three times, then adds water — exactly to the first knuckle of her finger. She says her mother taught her this. Rice cooked in an iron pot has a crispy bottom layer — the crust is crunchy and fragrant. As a child that crust was my favorite. Grandmother always saved it for me, eating the softer rice on top herself. After growing up and working in the city, I cook with a rice cooker — the taste is always rather ordinary. Once I went home and Grandmother made iron-pot rice again. I took a bite of the crust and suddenly my eyes stung. Not because it was delicious, but because that taste reminded me of the childhood kitchen, and Grandmother standing in it.

questions.q_zh: 外婆用什么做米饭？
questions.q_en: What does Grandmother use to cook rice?

questions.q_zh: 外婆每次把什么留给「我」？
questions.q_en: What does Grandmother always save for 'me'?

questions.q_zh: 「我」回家吃锅巴的时候为什么眼睛酸？
questions.q_en: Why did 'my' eyes sting when eating the crust at home?

options.text: 那个味道让「我」想起了小时候和外婆
options.pinyin: nàge wèidào ràng wǒ xiǎng qǐ le xiǎo shíhou hé wàipó
options.text_en: the taste reminded me of childhood and Grandmother

title: The 24-Hour Laundromat
text_zh: 我家旁边有一家二十四小时的自助洗衣店。白天来的人比较多，大多是学生和上班的年轻人。但是深夜来的人不一样——有刚下夜班的护士，有跑了一天的外卖员，有抱着一堆衣服的单身爸爸。洗衣机转的时候，大家坐在塑料椅子上等。那三十分钟是一段比较奇怪的时间——太短了不值得回家，太长了又有点无聊。我看到那个单身爸爸一边等洗衣服一边给孩子打电话：「爸爸很快就回来了，你先睡。」洗衣机停了，他把衣服叠好，走了。那家店比较旧了，灯也不太亮。但是对很多人来说，它是深夜最后一个开着门的地方。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā pángbiān yǒu yì jiā èrshísì xiǎoshí de zìzhù xǐyī diàn. Báitiān lái de rén bǐjiào duō, dàduō shì xuéshēng hé shàngbān de niánqīng rén. Dànshì shēnyè lái de rén bù yíyàng——yǒu gāng xià yèbān de hùshi, yǒu pǎo le yì tiān de wàimàiyuán, yǒu bàozhe yì duī yīfu de dānshēn bàba. Xǐyījī zhuǎn de shíhou, dàjiā zuò zài sùliào yǐzi shàng děng. Nà sānshí fēnzhōng shì yí duàn bǐjiào qíguài de shíjiān——tài duǎn le bù zhíde huí jiā, tài cháng le yòu yǒudiǎn wúliáo. Wǒ kàn dào nàge dānshēn bàba yìbiān děng xǐ yīfu yìbiān gěi háizi dǎ diànhuà:「Bàba hěn kuài jiù huílái le, nǐ xiān shuì.」Xǐyījī tíng le, tā bǎ yīfu dié hǎo, zǒu le. Nà jiā diàn bǐjiào jiù le, dēng yě bú tài liàng. Dànshì duì hěn duō rén lái shuō, tā shì shēnyè zuìhòu yí gè kāizhe mén de dìfang.
text_en: Next to my place there's a 24-hour self-service laundromat. Daytime customers are mostly students and young workers. But late-night visitors are different — nurses just off night shift, delivery workers who've been running all day, single fathers carrying piles of clothes. While the machines spin, everyone sits on plastic chairs waiting. Those thirty minutes are a rather strange stretch — too short to go home, too long to not be bored. I saw the single father call his child while waiting for laundry: 'Dad will be home soon, go to sleep first.' The machine stopped, he folded the clothes, and left. The shop is rather old, the lights not very bright. But for many people, it's the last place with an open door late at night.

questions.q_zh: 深夜来洗衣店的都是什么人？
questions.q_en: Who comes to the laundromat late at night?

options.text: 下夜班的护士、外卖员、单身爸爸
options.pinyin: xià yèbān de hùshi, wàimàiyuán, dānshēn bàba
options.text_en: off-shift nurses, delivery workers, single fathers

questions.q_zh: 单身爸爸等洗衣服的时候在做什么？
questions.q_en: What was the single father doing while waiting?

questions.q_zh: 对很多人来说，这家店是什么？
questions.q_en: What is this shop to many people?

options.text: 深夜最后一个开着门的地方
options.pinyin: shēnyè zuìhòu yí gè kāizhe mén de dìfang
options.text_en: the last place open late at night

title: The Sound of Morning
text_zh: 如果你五点半醒来，城市还在睡觉。但是如果你仔细听，你会发现早晨有很多声音。五点半的时候，环卫工人的扫地声是第一个声音——沙沙沙的，很有节奏。五点四十五分左右，第一班公交车开过去了，引擎的声音低低的。六点钟的时候，早餐摊开始了，油锅的声音滋滋响。六点半，遛狗的人出来了，狗的指甲在地上哒哒哒。七点，小区里的灯一盏一盏地亮了。我有一段时间每天五点半醒来，不是因为闹钟，而是因为窗外那些声音叫醒了我。我后来才明白，城市从来不是被阳光叫醒的——它是被人叫醒的。
text_pinyin: Rúguǒ nǐ wǔ diǎn bàn xǐng lái, chéngshì hái zài shuì jiào. Dànshì rúguǒ nǐ zǐxì tīng, nǐ huì fāxiàn zǎochén yǒu hěn duō shēngyīn. Wǔ diǎn bàn de shíhou, huánwèi gōngrén de sǎo dì shēng shì dì-yī gè shēngyīn——shāshāshā de, hěn yǒu jiézòu. Wǔ diǎn sìshíwǔ fēn zuǒyòu, dì-yī bān gōngjiāo chē kāi guòqu le, yǐnqíng de shēngyīn dī dī de. Liù diǎn zhōng de shíhou, zǎocān tān kāishǐ le, yóu guō de shēngyīn zīzī xiǎng. Liù diǎn bàn, liú gǒu de rén chūlái le, gǒu de zhǐjia zài dì shàng dādādā. Qī diǎn, xiǎoqū lǐ de dēng yì zhǎn yì zhǎn de liàng le. Wǒ yǒu yí duàn shíjiān měi tiān wǔ diǎn bàn xǐng lái, búshì yīnwèi nàozhōng, ér shì yīnwèi chuāng wài nàxiē shēngyīn jiào xǐng le wǒ. Wǒ hòulái cái míngbai, chéngshì cónglái búshì bèi yángguāng jiào xǐng de——tā shì bèi rén jiào xǐng de.
text_en: If you wake at five-thirty, the city is still sleeping. But if you listen carefully, you'll find the morning has many sounds. At five-thirty, the sweeping of sanitation workers is the first sound — swish swish swish, very rhythmic. Around five-forty-five, the first bus passes, its engine humming low. At six, breakfast stalls start up, oil sizzling in the pan. At six-thirty, dog walkers come out, dog nails clicking on the ground. At seven, the lights in the complex turn on one by one. For a while I woke at five-thirty every day — not because of an alarm, but because those sounds outside the window woke me. I later understood: the city is never woken by sunlight — it's woken by people.

questions.q_zh: 早晨第一个声音是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the first morning sound?

options.pinyin: huánwèi gōngrén sǎo dì de shēngyīn

questions.q_zh: 六点钟的时候能听到什么？
questions.q_en: What can you hear at six o'clock?

options.pinyin: zǎocān tān yóu guō de shēngyīn

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得城市是被什么叫醒的？
questions.q_en: What does 'I' think wakes the city?
